tool.name: RGI main
tool.description: identify antimicrobial resistance genes using CARD
tool.command: mkdir -p staging && rgi load @CMD_CARD_LOAD_ARGS@ --local && rgi main -i '$input_sequence' -o staging/output -t '$input_type' -a '$alignment_tool' -n \${GALAXY_SLOTS:-4} -d '$data_type' #if str($input_type) == "contig" -g '$orf_options.orf_finder' #end if #if str($input_type) == "contig" $orf_options.split_prodigal_jobs #end if $hit_inclusion.include_loose $hit_inclusion.include_nudge $hit_inclusion.low_quality --local --clean

param.name: input_sequence
param.label: Input sequences
param.help: FASTA or gzip-compressed FASTA file containing contigs or protein sequences.

param.name: input_type
param.label: Input type
param.help: Whether the input sequences are nucleotide contigs or translated protein sequences.

param.name: alignment_tool
param.label: Alignment tool
param.help: DIAMOND is substantially faster for large inputs; BLAST is more sensitive.

param.name: data_type
param.label: Sequence data type
param.help: Genomic context of the input sequences. Affects result reporting categories.

param.name: orf_finder
param.label: ORF finding tool
param.help: Only applies when input type is Contig. PYRODIGAL is a faster Python reimplementation of PRODIGAL.

param.label: Split Prodigal jobs
param.help: Run multiple Prodigal ORF prediction jobs simultaneously, one per contig. Only applies when input type is Contig. Useful for assemblies with many contigs when multiple CPU cores are available.

param.label: Include loose hits
param.help: Report hits that only partially meet curated CARD model thresholds. Increases sensitivity at the cost of specificity.

param.label: Include nudged hits (loose promoted to strict)
param.help: Include hits nudged from Loose to Strict based on RGI's internal algorithm.

param.label: Low quality / short contigs mode
param.help: Use for assemblies with short or partial gene sequences. Adjusts ORF detection thresholds accordingly.

tool.help:
**RGI main — Resistance Gene Identifier**
Identifies antimicrobial resistance (AMR) genes and mutations in genomic or protein sequences by aligning them against the Comprehensive Antibiotic Resistance Database (CARD).
-----
**Inputs**
- **Input sequences** — FASTA file of contigs (nucleotide) or protein sequences. gzip-compressed FASTA is accepted. - **CARD database** — Either select a pre-installed CARD release (set up by your Galaxy administrator) or supply a ``card.json`` file from your history. Download the latest release from https://card.mcmaster.ca/download.
-----
**Input type**
- *Contig* (default) — nucleotide sequences. RGI uses PRODIGAL or PYRODIGAL to predict ORFs before alignment. - *Protein* — translated amino acid sequences. ORF prediction is skipped.
-----
**Alignment tool**
- *DIAMOND* (default) — much faster for large inputs (thousands of contigs); slightly reduced sensitivity compared to BLAST. - *BLAST* — more sensitive; use when thoroughness matters more than speed.
-----
**Hit inclusion options**
By default, RGI reports only **Perfect** and **Strict** hits — sequences that meet curated CARD model thresholds.
- *Include loose hits* — also reports Loose hits that partially satisfy model thresholds. Useful for exploratory analysis; increases false-positive rate. - *Include nudged hits* — reports Loose hits that RGI promotes to Strict based on contextual evidence. - *Low quality / short contigs* — adjusts ORF detection for assemblies with truncated or partial genes (e.g., short-read assemblies).
-----
**Outputs**
- **AMR results (tabular)** — tab-delimited summary of all detected AMR genes, including ORF coordinates, CARD ARO accession, drug class, resistance mechanism, and alignment scores. - **AMR results (JSON)** — full JSON output with all hit details, suitable for downstream parsing or visualization with the CARD web interface.
-----
**References**
Alcock et al. (2020). CARD 2020: antibiotic resistome surveillance with the Comprehensive Antibiotic Resistance Database. *Nucleic Acids Research*, 48(D1), D517–D525. doi:10.1093/nar/gkz935
RGI GitHub: https://github.com/arpcard/rgi
